summary: Verified simplified signup/login UI (Google + OTP only, no password mode) and OTP backend flows. All 10 backend tests pass. Frontend signup OTP flow → /dashboard works; login OTP flow → /dashboard works. No forbidden password-mode testids remain. 'Emergent' word does NOT appear in user-visible text on signup or login pages (only inside auth.emergentagent.com URL string in code, which is required for OAuth redirect).
passed_tests: Backend POST /api/auth/google/session with invalid session_id -> 401 'Invalid session'
Backend POST /api/auth/otp/verify with correct OTP -> 200 phone_verified=true
Backend duplicate phone signup -> 400 'पहले से रजिस्टर्ड है'
Backend POST /api/auth/login-otp/send + verify for registered phone -> 200 with token
Frontend /signup shows: signup-google-btn + otp-name-input + otp-phone-input + send-otp-button (all data-testids present)
Frontend /signup does NOT have testids signup-mode-otp / signup-mode-password / password-signup-btn / password-name-input
Frontend /login does NOT have password-mode testids
Frontend signup OTP flow: name+phone -> OTP step -> verified OTP -> /dashboard with success toast (अकाउंट बन गया!)
Frontend login OTP flow: phone -> OTP step -> verified OTP -> /dashboard with success toast (लॉगिन सफल!)
Frontend src grep: 'Emergent' literal appears ONLY inside 'auth.emergentagent.com' URL strings (code, not user-visible JSX text)
Visible body text on /signup and /login does not contain 'emergent' (case-insensitive)
critical_code_review_comments: Note: Two separate google-session endpoints exist in auth_routes.py: /auth/google-session (line 95, used nowhere?) and /auth/google/session (line 499, used by AuthCallback.js). Consider deleting the unused one to avoid drift. Both behave similarly but only one is wired up.

Legacy endpoints /auth/register, /auth/login, /auth/register-mobile remain in backend but are no longer invoked by frontend. Per main agent note this is intentional for legacy users; consider deprecation comments.
auth_routes.py is ~555 lines - close to the 700-line guideline cap; consider splitting into auth_routes_password.py / auth_routes_otp.py / auth_routes_oauth.py if more endpoints land.
OTP store is in-memory dict (config.otp_store). Acceptable for single-worker deployment, but multi-worker uvicorn would lose OTPs across workers. Consider Redis if scaling.

test_credentials.test_user_signup_phone_used: 9878325593 (created live during signup flow test, registered as TestUser)

seed_data_creation: Created TEST_OtpUser via OTP signup with rolling phone 98765XXXXX (epoch-based) for backend test, plus 9878325593 via UI signup; both phones now exist in users collection.
context_for_next_testing_agent: All targeted requirements pass. SignupPage.js and LoginPage.js are fully simplified to (Google + OTP) only. AuthCallback uses /api/auth/google/session which validates session_id. Backend test file at /app/backend/tests/test_auth_otp_google.py can be reused. To test Google OAuth callback end-to-end, would need real session_id from Emergent auth — backend endpoint correctness already verified with bad session_ids (400/401).
